cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this step by step. So the question is asking what Mr. Williams was doing when he was stopped by the policeman. The options are A to D. First, let me recap the story. Mr. Williams comes to a crossroads with a "SLOW" sign. He slows down, looks both ways, and then drives across without stopping completely. Then a policeman stops him and says he didn't stop. Mr. Williams responds that the sign only says SLOW, not STOP. The policeman then realizes he's in the wrong street. The key here is the sequence of actions. The sign says SLOW. Mr. Williams slows down but doesn't stop. According to the story, at crossroads with a SLOW sign, drivers don't have to stop unless there's something coming. So he slowed down (which he did) and then drove across without stopping. So when the policeman stopped him, what was he doing? The policeman says he didn't stop at the crossing. Mr. Williams says he did go slow. The policeman then checks and realizes the sign is SLOW, but then the officer says he's in the wrong street. So maybe the officer was under the impression that the sign was a STOP sign and was watching the wrong road. Now, the question is about what Mr. Williams was doing when he was stopped. The options are: A. High speed - but he slowed down, so probably not. B. Driving onto the main road - Well, the story says he came to the main road. When he got to the main road, he slowed. But the exact timing is when he was on the main road. The sign says to stop if it's a STOP sign, but he was on the main road.
